evals.prompt: I'm about to launch https://solomax.app publicly tomorrow. Can you check if I'm actually ready?
evals.expected_output: A launch readiness report combining automated checks (run via scripts/check_site.sh: HTTPS, meta/OG tags, robots, sitemap, 404) with the manual checklist sections (flows, trust, emails, copy), organized into Ready / Blockers / Nice-to-fix with a launch verdict.
assertions: The automated site check script is run against the URL (evidence of its output in the response)
The report is organized into passing items, blockers, and nice-to-fix categories
A clear launch verdict is given (ready / fix blockers first / hold)
Items that cannot be checked automatically (e.g. email deliverability, payment flow) are listed as questions or manual steps rather than silently skipped

evals.prompt: pre-launch audit please. my app is a meal-planning PWA, not deployed yet, runs on localhost:3000. stripe is set up in test mode. no privacy policy yet.
evals.expected_output: A checklist-driven audit adapted to a not-yet-deployed app: flags missing privacy policy as a blocker (payments + user data), notes Stripe test→live checklist, lists what can only be verified after deploy, and gives a prioritized fix list.
assertions: The missing privacy policy is flagged as a launch blocker given payments are collected
Stripe test-mode to live-mode transition is addressed
The output distinguishes checks possible now vs. checks requiring the deployed URL
Fixes are prioritized by impact rather than presented as a flat list
